entry.title: Re: Czech Republic's Currency
entry.content:
CZK currency is still possible to use as main currency according to § 24a letter a) of The accounting law no. 563/1991 Coll. According to the § 24a letter b) is possible to use other foreign currencies as main currency if the currency is chosen as functional currency. Allowed foreign currencies are 1. Euro, 2. USD and 3. GBP.

This means that if the chosen functional currency is EUR, this needs to be currency of the main company code. SAP supports the functionality of the functional currency documented in SAP help portal S/4HANA ON-PREMISE and in S/4HANA Cloud .

entry.title: Re: Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 1 - Concept
entry.content:
@PrasanthiBherthepudi Probably yes. If you have enabled authenticationMethod: "route" and have enabled authenticationType: "xsuaa", it should trigger the authentication flow.

Regarding the destination check, this is the expected behaviour as AI Core responds with 404 for /v2 endpoints.

entry.title: Further evolution of MRP apps "Monitor Material Coverage"
entry.content:
We are constantly enhancing the MRP apps so that production planners can evaluate the MRP results with the following FIORI apps: 

  1. Monitor Material Coverage - Net Segments (F0247A)

  2. Monitor Material Coverage - Net and Individual Segments (F2101A)

Here are the details of the latest innovations. Check out the enhanced the SAP Fiori look & feel with its real-time data evaluation. With that you will have your planning situations always under control. 

In total we enhanced the apps with the following additional columns, navigations and colorings:

If you are interested in the details continue to read below. 

Master Data Issues 

If master data issues are detected for a material during an MRP run, the number of issues is displayed in this column:

By clicking on the link, you can check the number of warnings, errors, and cancellations that have been identified, and navigate to the Display MRP Master Data Issues app to display detailed information and solve or accept the issues as required. 

When you navigate to the Display MRP Master Data Issues app, the list of master data issues is filtered automatically according to the category and the respective material that you selected (in my case I selected "Errors"): 

Past Due Elements 

This column displays the number of receipt elements in the stock / requirements list that have an opening date, a basic start date / release date, or a basic end date / delivery date in the past:

Depending on the type of an MRP receipt element, different dates can be defined to specify when an open quantity is planned to be delivered. For example, a planned order has an opening date, a basic start date, and a basic end date, while a purchase requisition has a release date and delivery date assigned. To determine the number of past due elements, the system adds up the following MRP elements:

  • Receipt elements that have an opening date in the past

  • Receipt elements that have a basic start date or release date in the past

  • Receipt elements that have a basic end date or delivery date in the past

The sum of these elements is displayed in this column to help you determine whether actions are required to make sure all requirements from the last MRP run can be fulfilled.

Important to know is that production orders or process orders that have a basic start date in the past, but a basic end date in the future are not considered for this column, because a start date in the past is not classified as an exception for these types of MRP elements.

Opening Day in Past 

This column displays the number of receipt elements in the stock / requirements list that have an opening date in the past:

The opening date of a receipt element is the date at which a procurement proposal (purchase requisition or planned order) should be converted into a purchase order or production order / process order. The responsible MRP controller can use the period between the opening date and the start date to complete the conversion. 

The system automatically counts the number of receipt elements with an opening date in the past and displays it in this column. This helps you determine whether actions are required to make sure the requirements that were determined during the last MRP run can be fulfilled.

Important to know is that for this column, the system only counts receipt elements that do not have a basic start date or a basic end date in the past. Receipt elements with a basic start date or a basic end date in the past are added to the counter in the Start Date in Past and End Date in Past columns, respectively.

Start Day in Past 

This column displays the number of receipt elements in the stock / requirements list that have a basic start date or release date in the past: 

The start date or release date of a receipt element is the earliest date at which the production or delivery of an ordered quantity can start. A start date in the past might indicate the conversion is already too late. In the example above it is one; therefore, the system indicates that we have one receipt element with a basic start date or release date in the past.

This is important for the production planner to help determine whether actions are required to make sure the requirements that were determined during the last MRP run can be fulfilled. Important to know is: 

  • For this column, the system only counts receipt elements that do not have a basic end date or delivery date in the past. Receipt elements with a basic end date in the past are added to the counter in the End Date in Past column. 

  • The opening date in the past is also not calculated. Always the worst possible past due date is counted.

  • Production orders and process orders are not taken into account for this column, because a start date in the past is not classified as an exception for these MRP element types.

End Date in Past 

This column displays the number of receipt elements in the stock / requirements list that have a basic end date or a delivery date in the past: 

The end date or delivery date of a receipt element is the latest date at which an ordered quantity must be available. If we look at material FIO-TSC-ACC0007 then we see four elements that have an end date in the past: 

The system automatically counts the number of receipt elements with a basic end date or delivery date in the past and displays it in this column. This helps you determine whether actions are required to make sure the requirements that were determined during the last MRP run can be fulfilled.

Rescheduling Proposals 

Companies want to avoid supply shortages or excess supply; therefore, they need to be able to reschedule receipt elements to an earlier date to prevent material shortages or to a later date, for example, if requirements are postponed. Furthermore, enterprises need to be able to cancel receipt elements if they are no longer required:

This is really easy to do now and you will find all the details in this Blog Post: Manage Supply Shortage and Excess Supply with MRP ... - SAP Community

Note

This column indicates whether a material note exists for a material:

For each material, you can create a material note to provide additional information to production planners. If a note exists for a material, Yes is displayed in this column. You can see the message of the note in the Manage Material Coverage app: 

With this it is really easy for the Production Planner to leave additional planning comments regarding the material.  

Day's Supply (Working Days)

As a production planner, you can use days' supply information to evaluate the current planning results for a selected material. Based on the current stock and the receipt and requirements information included in the stock / requirements list for a material, the system determines the day of the first shortage and displays the number of working days until the shortage occurs in this column:

For shortages that occur in the future, the system counts the number of working days that can be fully covered and displays it as a positive value. Neither the current date nor the day of the first shortage are taken into account for this calculation.

 

For shortages that occur today or in the past, the system counts the number of working days between the day of the first shortage and the current date and displays it as a negative value. The day of the first shortage and the current date are taken into account for this calculation, that is, a shortage that occurs today results in a days' supply value of -1.

 

The values displayed in this column can differ according to the shortage definition that you select.

For materials with time-dependent safety stock levels, the system automatically compares the number of working days determined as days' supply with the Safety Days of Supply (Safety DOS) and Target Days of Supply (Target DOS) values that are defined in the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels app for the current date. The definition can be done material specific and time-interval specific: 

 Based on the result of this comparison, it adapts the coloring of the days' supply value as follows:

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is below the safety DOS, the value in the Days' Supply column is highlighted in red.

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is equal to the safety DOS or between the safety DOS and the target DOS, the value in the Days' Supply column is highlighted in green.

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is equal to or above the target DOS, the value in the Days' Supply column is highlighted in yellow.

The reason behind the coloring is to avoid shortage situations (below safety stock) and highlight the overstock (over target). The goal for the planner is to keep the inventory on the green level between safety and the target. 

The color coding is applied to all materials within the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Level app, regardless of the safety stock method selected in planning or whether the time-dependent values are factored into the planning process.

Stock Day's Supply (Working Days)

Displays the number of working days that can be fully covered before a shortage occurs for the selected material, based on its current stock. Planned receipts are not taken into account:

 

As a production planner, you can use stock days' supply information to evaluate the current planning results for a selected material. Based on the current stock and the requirement information included in the stock / requirements list for a material, the system determines the day of the first shortage and displays the number of working days until the shortage occurs in this column:

  • For shortages that occur in the future, the system counts the number of working days that can be fully covered and displays it as a positive value. Neither the current date nor the day of the first shortage are taken into account for this calculation.

  • For shortages that occur today or in the past, the system counts the number of working days between the day of the first shortage and the current date and displays it as a negative value. The day of the first shortage and the current date are taken into account for this calculation, that is, a shortage that occurs today results in a stock days' supply value of -1.

The values displayed in this column can differ according to the shortage definition that you select.

The same dependencies apply also here with the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels app for the current date. 

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is below the safety DOS, the value in the Stock Days' Supply column is highlighted in red.

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is equal to the safety DOS or between the safety DOS and the target DOS, the value in the Stock Days' Supply column is highlighted in green.

  • If the number of working days until the first shortage is equal to or above the target DOS, the value in the Stock Days' Supply column is highlighted in yellow.

Intraday Supply (Working Days) 

Displays the number of working days that can be fully covered before a shortage occurs for a material, plus two decimal places to indicate which fraction of the requirements on the day of the first shortage can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. The intraday supply is determined based on the current stock and the receipts and requirements information included in the stock / requirements list for a material.

As a production planner, you can use intraday supply information to evaluate the current planning results for a selected material. The value displayed in this column includes the following information:

  • Days' supply: the number of full working days until a shortage occurs

 

  • Fractional coverage of the requirements on the day of the first shortage: fraction of the requirements that can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. It is calculated by dividing the available quantity by the aggregated days' demand.

    The system determines the value for this column based on the following formula: Intraday Supply = Days’ Supply + Fractional Coverage on the Day of the First Shortage.

The value displayed in this column can differ according to the shortage definition that you select.

 

For materials with the values defined in time-dependent safety stock levels (MTDSL) for the current date, the system automatically compares the number of full working days until the first shortage (days' supply) with the Safety DOS and Target DOS values that are defined in the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels app for the current date. Based on the result of this comparison, it adapts the coloring of the intraday supply value as follows:

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage (days' supply) is below the safety DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in red.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to the safety DOS or between the safety DOS and the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in green.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to or above the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in yellow.

The coloring of the intraday supply value is determined based on full working days. The fractional coverage on the day of the first shortage is not taken into account. So, the coloring is the same as for Days Supply. 

Stock Intraday Supply (Working Days)

Displays the number of working days until a shortage occurs for a material, plus two decimal places to indicate which fraction of the requirements on the day of the first shortage can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. The stock intraday supply is determined based on the current stock and the requirements information included in the stock / requirements list for a material. Planned receipts are not taken into account.

 

As a production planner, you can use stock intraday supply information to evaluate the current planning results for a selected material. The value displayed in this column includes the following information:

  • Stock days' supply: the number of full working days until a shortage occurs, based on the current stock of a material

  • Fractional coverage of the requirements on the day of the first shortage: fraction of the requirements that can be covered by the available quantity from the previous day. It is calculated by dividing the available quantity by the aggregated days' demand.

    The system determines the value for this column based on the following formula: Stock Intraday Supply = Stock Days’ Supply + Fractional Coverage on the Day of the First Shortage.

The value displayed in this column can differ according to the shortage definition that you select.

For materials which have values defined in the time-dependent safety stock levels, the system automatically compares the number of full working days until the first shortage (stock days' supply) with the Safety DOS and Target DOS values that are defined in the Maintain Time-Dependent Stock Levels app for the current date. Based on the result of this comparison, it adapts the coloring of the stock intraday supply value as follows:

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage (stock days' supply) is below the safety DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in red.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to the safety DOS or between the safety DOS and the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in green.

  • If the number of full working days until the first shortage is equal to or above the target DOS, the value in the Intraday Supply column is highlighted in yellow.

The coloring of the stock intraday supply value is determined based on full working days. The fractional coverage on the day of the first shortage is not taken into account. Also here the same principal as for Stock days supply. 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our new community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn . 

 

Watch Our Live Sessions

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

 

Where to Find More Information:

  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 Early Release Series here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/watch-the-replays-of-our-excl...
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDnnyN2j-CcUheNN-GaNCb3H
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel here: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset
  • Find out how to switch from groups to spaces and pages here: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/11/06/deactivation-of-groups-in-the-sap-fiori-launchpad-in-sap-s-4hana-cl...
  • Become an early adopter for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3612
  • Check out the SAP Activate Roadmap for early adopters here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapOvervie...
  • Review the PPL content here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContent...
  • Listen to our podcast here: https://podcast.opensap.info/inside-sap/
  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace here: https://microlearning.opensap.com/
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/latest?region=DE
  • Check out what’s new here: https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226  
  • Find help here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD
  • Read the feature scope description here: https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 10 why items weight and size are mandatory?
entry.content:
Hi,

We didn't change the item creation validations in SAP B1 V10 and we didn't expose any new configuration that could affect this area. We only exposed the system settings in the web client without changing the existing system behavior.

Please check maybe a customization was done by the partner.

Regards, Ofrit.

entry.title: Re: Exception during start of deployment for deploy type...
entry.content:
When using Basic Authentication the connection check in the destination UI should return '200 OK' (one of the rare cases where this check works...). However, this just ensures that the user can authenticate towards the subaccount. It does not check the needed authorizations in the target.

To be on the safe side you could make the user a member of the CF organisation in which the target space is located (Org User should be sufficient).

Additionally, the user in the destination has to be a platform user (see here: https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/platform-users).

Last not least, you need an additional destination called 'CloudIntegration' in the target subaccount (see here: https://help.sap.com/docs/content-agent-service/user-guide/create-sap-cloud-integration-destination)

Alternatively, you could use Content Agent Service for deploying Cloud Integration content (instead of the CF Deploy service). This is described here: https://help.sap.com/docs/content-agent-service/user-guide/create-target-node-destination

Please note that you cannot deploy CF extension applications (developed for example in Business Application Studio) via Content Agent Service. This is only possible with the deploy service.

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP Business Connector to Solution Manager...
entry.content:
Hello Symon,

business Connector is used elster e.g. . You can connect any system with SMDAGENT or saphostagent to solman also non sap products like business objects, tomcat , websphere ....

there are several notes/guides for managed system setup on solution manager for example here for tomcat (i think bc also runs on a tomcat)

1633036 - SAP Solution Manager 7.2 E2E RCA Setup for Apache Tomcat

 

furthermore introscope agent is a plugin for solution manager (wily ca). so you need to look for /usr/sap/<SID/ on solution manager system. there should be the installation of wily introscope, but you can install it where ever you want. 

you should first check if you can reach wily  introscope over weburl and if it is correctly integrated in your solution manager system. after that you can go on with managed system setup of the bc.

br

robert

entry.title: Re: Migration of Webi reports to Oracle analytics cloud
entry.content: Have you developed any reports from scratch in OAC? or created semantic model in OAC?

entry.title: Re: Fiori List Report displays identical records despite underlying CDS View containing distinct dat
entry.content: check what the odata returns you.

entry.title: Public Cloud - edit actual cost rate in control code currency
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

After financial closing, I want to edit my actual cost rate in company code currency. I am testing.

"manage cost rates - actual" app keeps company code currency. I edited and closed the cockpit again.

My edit effected to control code currency cost rate too. But EUR and TRY is different currencies. I want edit only one. How could I do?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 1 - Concept
entry.content:
Hello @merbinjanselm  thanks for the great tutorial!

I have the same setup for point 6. Bonus: How to configure during development
and ui5-middleware-approuter is working fine locally in VS code, but in SAP Business Application Studio callback URLs for the redirection are not working fine when i try to open the app on localhost:8080 (https://port8080-workspaces-ws-cbxrd.eu10.applicationstudio.cloud.sap)
it stuck on 
https://<scrubbed>.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=<scrubbed>&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fport8080-workspaces-ws-cbxrd.eu10.applicationstudio.cloud.sap%2Flogin%2Fcallback

with the message: "uh oh something went amiss"
I got same message even if i try to access the approuter itself, which is hosted by default on port 5000, from VS code I'm able to  to hit my cf odata endpoints from the local approuter, but in BAS it stuck on the callback url.

Do you have any hints to configure it to work in BAS as well?

entry.title: Re: Missing purpose string in Info.plist
entry.content:
How did you solve the iusse "Asset validation failed (90135)" ?

Invalid bundle. The executable couldn’t be re-signed for upload to App Store Connect. The app may have been built or signed with unsupported or prerelease tools.

My App has same problem, and i totally have no clue to find where am i wrong....  cry....

entry.title: SAC Advanced Formula - Copy month value to all months of specific year
entry.content:
Hello,

the requirement is rather simple, but i am not yet very experience in advanced formulas.

I want to select a month of a specific year (defined as dynamic parameter) and then select a year (or year as the node of a date hierarchy). 

Then this monthly value should be copied to every month of the selected year. 

How is this possible with advanced formula?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Testing Groovy Script Locally using Intellij IDEA
entry.content:
Hi @shoaib-alam !

I followed your steps and I want to appreciate your work, it works very well! Thank you very much 🙂 

When I try to download the stack.worker_cf.karaf-6.xx.x.war (currently 6.53.11) I get an out of memory error. 
Could some help me with getting the neccessary .jar-Files?

Greetings
jonas

entry.title: Re: Nota Técnica 2023.001: tributação monofásica sobre combustíveis
entry.content:
Olá @luize_boyen.

Ao aplicar a nota 3311652 (Outbound NF-e: Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3311653), me deparei com o erro na imagem abaixo:

Informações adicionais do erro:

"What happened? - The metaddta for database connection "R/3*DDFTX" could not be found due to error 99 in the Database Shared Library (DBSL)."

" Information on where terminated - The termination occurred in ABAP program or include "SAPLSDIFRUNTIME", in "DD_INT_DELETE_DDFTX_RANGE". The main program was "SCWN_NOTE_BROWSER".  In the source code, the termination point is in line 21 of program or include "LSDIFRUNTIMEU06". "

Informações do ambiente SAP:

Componente | Release | SP-Level | Support Package
SAP_BASIS  | 750         | 0009      | SAPK-75009INSAPBASIS
SAP_APPL   | 618         | 0008      | SAPK-61808INSAPAPPL

Por favor, poderia me auxiliar como corrigir? Seria o caso de solicitar apoio a BASIS?

Agradeço desde já.

entry.title: Introducing the Organizational Change Management Cross Topic in the SAP Activate Methodology
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that Organizational Change Management (OCM) is now available as a cross topic in the Roadmap Viewer. This cross topic serves as an invaluable resource for organizations embarking on SAP projects, offering a systematic approach to effectively manage the people side of such transformations.

The success of an SAP implementation is not solely dependent on the technical aspects, but also heavily relies on how well the human aspects are addressed. This includes providing information, managing expectations, reducing concerns, and ensuring all stakeholders are on board with the upcoming changes. Within the OCM cross topic, you will find a wealth of resources, hands-on tools and methodologies designed to support every phase of your project – from the initial planning stage to post-implementation monitoring. The OCM cross topic is fully aligned with SAP’s implementation methodology, SAP Activate. The chart below illustrates the alignment of all OCM deliverables with the Activate phases.

To dive deeper into how the six dimensions of OCM support the entire implementation lifecycle by addressing all relevant action areas, please view the OCM cross topic here.

As SAP solutions continue to evolve, OCM remains a key success factor for every SAP project, fostering a smooth transition and supporting a fast and sustainable user adoption. Whether you are just starting your SAP implementation journey or are already deep into the process, the OCM cross topic offers the guidance and tools you need to manage change effectively.

If you would like more information on OCM, check out the free learning journey Discover Organizational Change Management for SAP Cloud Projects.

entry.title: SAP MM - Mandatory characteristics, possibility to hide?
entry.content:
So we have Cross plant CM's and in there are configurations of our goods.

I am looking for an solution for the following.

We have a characteristic, if you choose this characteristic (Yes) then 4 extra fields will appear.
If you choose this characteristic (No) the fields will be hidden. 
So far so good.

Now we want the fields that appear after choosing 'Yes' to be filled in mandatory. They cannot be left empty.
The problem I have now is that if we make the fields mandatory, they remain mandatory even when they are hidden (choice 'No').

Does anyone have a solution for this?

entry.title: Error in price calculation in outbound delivery due to field KOMK-KUNNR
entry.content:
Dear experts,

in my outbound delivery I use a pricing condition (for freight costs) with an access sequence, that uses the sold-to customer number (see screenshot, so far nothing special...), but during outbound delivery processing KOMK-KUNNR will have different values depending on delivery creation or delivery change. Therefore it's obligatory to have at least one partner role that deviates from the sold-to party, e.g. the goods receiver is not the same as sold-to.

This miscalculation of the customer number leads to wrong results in pricing...

From an abap point of view the logic for filling komk-kunnr is in the program SAPFV50P, in include FV50PF0P_P_KOMK_KOMP_FUELLEN in form routine P_KOMK_KOMP_FUELLEN.

Here komk-kunnr is filled from kuagv-kunnr, which is prefilled with the correct value during document creation (VL01N). During document change (VL02N) it gets determined in routine auftraggeber_bereitstellen, which therefore uses an old internal table with headerline called xvbpa[].

Up to S/4HANA 2021 this was fine, because they used a loop ... where parvw = parvw_ag, but in S/4 2022 and higher they replaced the loop with new abap-objects stuff, but they forgot about remainig data in the header line of xvbpa...

Can someone confirm this faulty behaviour in their S/4HANA system?

Best regards,

Mark

entry.title: Process like TAC KSA3 available in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I was wondering if the Actual Accrual for Cost Centers is somehow available in S4  Public Cloud because my customer uses it regularly. I can not find anything similar except of the accruals in materials management. Does anyone know if there is an alternative process or something replacing this?  What should a customer do after implementing S/4HANA Cloud???

Thanks in advance for any hint!

Best regards

Rado

entry.title: How to export template from Solman
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,
Need your help to export Template from Solman, need to import this in other Solman system. 
 
Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to export template from Solman
entry.content:
Dear customer

The only possibility to transport templates is making usage of transport requests.

Please see KBA 2940033 - How to move custom templates from old Solution Manager system to new system

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAC Advanced Formula - Copy month value to all months...
entry.content:
I just figured it out myself, this is the code:

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "VALUE_PROJECT"

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YM], %TARGET_YEAR%)

DATA()=RESULTLOOKUP ( [d/Date] = %SOURCE_MONTH%)

entry.title: Re: Copying Inspection Results After a Usage Decision
entry.content: Hi, just to understand it better. You wrote "My client used the "Copy Inspection Results" option in their S4Hana On-premises system even after the UD had been made." Am I correct in assuming that the usage decision has already been made for the target inspection lot and that characteristics from a source inspection lot should then be copied into exactly this (completed) lot?

entry.title: Re: Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 1 - Concept
entry.content:
@GeCa You might be missing proper redirect-uris in xs-security.json

{
  ...
  "oauth2-configuration": {
    "redirect-uris": [
      // configure redirect uri
      "https://*.eu10.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/**",
      // or simply
      "https://*.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/**",
      // for trial accounts
      "https://*.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/**",
      // for local
      "http://localhost:8080/**"
    ]
  }
}

entry.title: Re: Project Settlement in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Craigie,

thanks for reading the blog and the feedback.
You can use the app "Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents" to change the revenue recognition key. From the list of service documents choose the one you want to change, on the detailed view click on the 3-dots on the upper right, and then choose "change recognition key".

Best regards, Birgit

entry.title: Re: Save the Date for Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
Hi @Matt_Sui,

Please follow the sign-up instructions in the blog post. It should work after.

Kind regards,
Maria

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI Sunset
entry.content: Any way to find out what SAP standard apps are based on Dynpro? I would like to know if SAP GUI is relevant in our green field implementation or we can decide not to use it

entry.title: Solman Charm RFC UI field modification
entry.content:
How we can disable / invisible a filed when we select standard change check box and also some filed should only hold single constant values.

Few blue cross fields need to be invisible when we select standard change check box.

Few yellow highlighted fields should have a single constant value when we are selecting standard change.

SOLMAN Change Control Management SAP Solution Manager User Interface

entry.title: Create Article Grouping VBG1 for brands or article hierarchy
entry.content:
Hello,

we want to use Bonus Buy for some use cases and would like to use Article Grouping to use brands (WRF_BRANDS) and article hierarchies (WRF_MATGRP_SKU). Is there any way or idea how we could do this? Currently it seems like the standards VBG1 solution only supports material groups (MARA-MATKL) which doesn't suite all our need. 

Any help or ideas are highly appreciated.

Kind regards,

entry.title: SAP Dynamic Forms 1.0 Released Features
entry.content:
Welcome to the release of SAP Dynamic Forms.  In this blog, we will introduce you to the product and go over the capabilities and key features in the initial release,.   

SAP Dynamic Forms is a new Software as a Service (SaaS) offering based on SAP Business Technology Platform allowing end users such as maintenance technicians to fill out digitized forms in the field and have that data written back to the SAP backend.  In the initial release, ECC and S/4HANA integration supports attachment of forms to work orders, notifications, equipment, and functional location.   Additional integration support such as seeing forms in service orders, service requests, or task list, is planned in future releases.    

The form builder allows form definitions to be created on the desktop in a drag-and-drop format.  This form designer allows you to set conditions in the forms, such as mandatory fields and custom formulas, so that complex calculation and threshold conditions can be created within the forms.   Additionally, the form runner, when configured, can pre-populate data from SAP objects.   In the initial release, we are supporting main PM objects such as Work Order, Notification, Equipment, Functional Location.   Support for additional objects will be coming in future releases, such as S/4 Service Order, maintenance plan/task list, APM, BNAC etc.       

There are two front ends in the initial release of SAP Dynamic forms.   When a form is created and published, it can be accessed via a link, this can then be used to create web-based Fiori tiles for access.   The mobile client is based on SAP Service and Asset Manager, minimal version 2310.   SAP Dynamic Forms is offered as an SAP Business Technology Platform service, so all forms created are saved in SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) and the forms use the SAP Mobile Application Integration Framework to interact with SAP backend. 

 SAP dynamic forms are seamlessly integrated with SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA backend through the configuration of forms to business objects in the form runner.      

SAP Dynamic Forms – Mobile User Journey Map  

This is a potential End to End Journey Map of those who would be involved in the creation and execution of a form in SAP Dynamic Forms. 

While the form designer is simple enough to be used by anyone in the office, mapping to business objects will require the support of a line of business experts, in order to pre-populate backend data in a published form using MAIF (Mobile Application Integration Framework).   Once a form is connected and published either through Automatic Object Linkage or Manual Object Linkage, the end user can conduct data capture and have it write back to create new business objects or stored for additional analytics during post processing.    

 SAP Dynamic Forms - Process Flow 

This is an in depth look at how the Mobile Application Integration Framework interacts with SAP Business Technology. 

Here is a sequence of events from an SAP Service and Asset Manager user perspective.  

  1. User sees list of maintenance order in SSAM (SAP Service & Asset Manager) that have forms associated with it through Automatic Form Creation in the new Dynamic Forms Agent. 
  1. User triggers initial data synch in SSAM with BTP, which can pre-populate data fields in the forms, so they are now ready to be used in SSAM offline mode. 
  1. The technician completes his work and fills out the form on the mobile device. 
  1. Once the SSAM user returns to an area with internet, he can trigger the SSAM Delta Synch, which writes back to the SAP backend though SAP BTP.   Data post processing can then be done after this.     

 Application Features  

Let us look at some of the features we are introducing in the initial release. For a more detailed listing, please refer to the official help documentation of SAP Dynamic Forms on the SAP help portal.   

Desktop – Form Designer Features 

SAP Dynamic Forms supports the following form designer features in the web-based desktop application.  

 

 

Form Builder – allowing you to design and create forms online 

  • Form Controls – interactive user interface elements allowing user to decide how to enter or view data in a form.  Examples include text field, date, time control, buttons and more. 
  • Selection Control – similar to Form Controls, instead of an open text entry, the user selects from predefined values.  Examples include drop downs, radio buttons, yes/no, and more.   
  • Grid Controls – allowing you to organize controls in rows and columns of a cell.  Examples include maintenance check list, inspection checklist and more.  
  • Data Validation and Alert Controls – To prevent incorrect data from being captured by the user, SAP Dynamic Forms supports the validation of data entered and alerts the user before submitting the form.  

Form Lifecycle Management 

  • Version Control 
  • View Utilization Data 
  • Import and Export 

Form Admin – Manage and perform admin tasks on your forms. 

Form Runner – Run and review your forms,  

Form Explorer – Manage the relationship between SAP business objects and the forms. Allows  

Template Generation – Create form templates and different form elements that you can reuse when creating your forms. 

ECC & S/4HANA Integration – You can attach your forms to the following objects: Work Orders, Notifications, Equipment, Functional Locations.   

Mobile – SAP Service and Asset Manager 

The following features are available in the offline-mobile solution SAP Service and Asset Manager, through the integration of SAP Dynamic Forms.  

 

Offline Form Utilization – when used with SAP Service and Asset Management, supports data input while offline, and data delta synch when online.   

Integration with Maintenance Persona in SSAM – In SAP Service and Asset Manager, you can utilize forms in this persona.   

Offline Validation – Form Formulas 

PDF Conversion – Ability to convert Forms into PDF 

Attached to Orders Ability to attached converted PDFs  

ECC and S/4HANA Integration – in SAP Service and Asset Manager, you can attach forms to Work Orders, Notifications, Equipment, Functional Locations 

Roadmap 

Please check out the product roadmap for future innovations in this space. 

Limitations 

Standalone usage of Forms via Fiori interface is planned for 2.0 release (Create Maintenance Request Find Maintenance Notification, Find Maintenance Order) 

Mobile usage of Forms requires SSAM version 2310 or higher.  

SSAM Windows version support is planned for 2.0 release. 

In Conclusion   

SAP Dynamic Forms is designed to help transform forms in asset management from paper to digitized version.   It brings simple to use form design functionality to the everyday business user and allows them to utilize digitized forms wherever they go, whether online or offline.       

You can try out SAP Dynamic Forms two ways, first, you can download the official SSAM App from the App Store or Google Play store with built in pre-loaded demo data, allowing you to check out how the forms will look like to a maintenance technician on a mobile device.   Second, you can go to the SAP Demo Experience environment and try out the form designer and create your own forms.        

Finally, feel free to provide feedback, comments or suggestions that will help us make the product even better.   

For licensing questions, please refer to the blog post by SAP Product Marketing, that will explain in more detail.

entry.title: Re: Ask for feedback
entry.content: Thank you for asking this, I have this same question!

entry.title: Re: CHARM Repack - Focused Build Standalone Extensions Part 2
entry.content:
Hi Stefan,

Thank you for the explanation of this tool. Part of what is presented is that you can repack external transports from vendors. I am the release manager and looking to implement this function.  Please correct or confirm my understanding of this use case.

My current process: Vendor transport is delivered from an external namespace to my development environment. My process would then require my Basis counterparts to create a General CD in Charm to manually import the vendor transport from DEV --> QAS --> VAL --> PRD.

Issues: No Charm advantages delivered. NO CSOL, NO DGP, NO Cross System checks.

Would repack tool process be the following?
Vendor transport delivered from external namespace. Basis counterparts import to DEV. Then use Normal Change Document and Repack tool to import via our normal process with all Charm Advantages?

Kinds Regards,
Matt

entry.title: I need SAP BTP SDK version 4.0.5. Can you please provide the link?
Re: Inventory cost adjustment
entry.content:
Hello,

if you change the inventory cost for the products, it will impact the Gain / Loss accounts in P&L based on the ADG and mapping of ADG to GLs in configurations. there is no way to avoid such postings. 

 

hence, if the number of transaction posted is small, you will need to reverse the transaction and update the cost and recreate them. (when you have 0 quantity in the inventory, you can change the cost and it would not have any impact in P&L. 0 Qty * cost = 0)

 

Regards,

Harshal

entry.title: Re: Process like TAC KSA3 available in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content: adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Finance

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud - edit actual cost rate in control code currency
entry.content: adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Finance

entry.title: Re: How to extract business configuration data from SAP B...
entry.content:
Hello,

Business Configuration work center,

reports view

Click on  Configuration Change History.

Go to Fine-tune settings, Show all activties

Look for the Finetune activity that you would like to export.

Untick Changes only tab on header table ( Changes only tab also exist on the details table)

Click on Download Changes Details


System will download the Zip file. Unzip the downloaded file and open the it,
in the exported excel, look for Sheet All Keys - All values

this should contain all the information of the finetune activities.
Note that the sheet is still grouped by the change / Deployed time stamp. So you will need to look for the latest one or consolidate the changes from various time stamps into one table ( making sure what is the relevant / correct values).

Regards,
Harshal

entry.title: BusinessObjects 4.3 SP4 - Patch 2/3/4/5 Issues
entry.content:
We are currently running BusinessObjects 4.3 SP2 - Patch 12 in our production environment.  We installed BusinessObjects 4.3 SP4 - Patch 3 on our sandbox and found a number of issues pertaining to different behavior from Webi in this patch compared to our current production version.  Significant enough differences for use to postpone upgrading to SP4 until a stable patch is released.

Has anyone else encountered issues with the BusinessObjects 4.3 SP4 (any patch level)?

What we found with BusinessObjects 4.3, SP4, Patch 3:

  1. We have a number of documents with multiple queries MERGED in the Webi document. We found the Extend Merged Dimension Values setting behaves differently in BOB43, SP4, Patch 3 compared to how it behaves in BOB43, SP2, Patch 12. SAP says the Extend Merged Dimension Values setting is “fixed” in the SP4, Patch 3 because it was “wrong” in previous versions. I am still going round with SAP on this because there is no documentation, that i can find or that they can provide, indicating this was found to be an issue and subsequently “fixed”.
  2. Auto Merge Dimensions setting is also behaving differently. If a user just opens a document and refreshes, Webi creates additional MERGED dimensions in the document. No editing of the document was done. Webi just created new MERGEs which, of course, caused issues with the document.
  3. In addition, we have also found some (not all) detail variables in the Webi document are losing their association with the dimension to which it is associated when the document is refreshed.

entry.title: Re: Error in Build process automation
entry.content:
@PatrikMajer : Thanks for your help earlier. In continuation with that I am in need with one more issue. Please extend your help here. 

I have created a destination in BTP and based on this destination created an action in the process automation. This action has input and output parameters in the body. 

input parameters:

output parameters:

And on testing the action from the test tab, i get required output. 

I added this action in a process in the Build process automation. As per my understanding, the input and output parameters of this action shown above, should be automatically added in the step of the process.

 

 

Although the input parameters get enabled and shown in the action display in the process, but the output parameter doesnt seem to be reflecting automatically here. 

I followed this link to implement the action part in the process automation, but in this link they have used standard S4HANA odata apis where as in my case I have not used a Odata api. Any suggestions what I might be doing wrong here?

entry.title: Employee Experience revisited: what really matters to drive meaningful impact in times of change
entry.content:
On 27th June 2024, SAP SuccessFactors Business Consultant Nicole Schadewald shared key insights from recent employee experience design projects at SAP in the customer webinar Employee Experience revisited: what really matters to drive meaningful impact in times of change.

Here are the key takeaways:

Employee Experience Meta Trends in 2024

As we enter 2024, the landscape of employee experience (EX) continues to evolve. Organizations are navigating a complex array of challenges, from the integration of AI to the necessity of fostering a supportive work environment. Let's explore the meta trends shaping the employee experience this year.

  1. AI and Automation: AI is set to revolutionize the workplace, driving productivity and transforming HR practices. While recruitment remains a key area, AI's impact will extend to training, development, and performance management.
  2. Skills Focus: Upskilling and reskilling are at the forefront, particularly with AI demanding new competencies. Organizations must prioritize workforce planning with AI integration in mind.
  3. Hybrid Work Evolution: Hybrid work remains prevalent, with a shift back towards office environments. Companies must create compelling in-office experiences while maintaining flexibility.
  4. DEI and Belonging: While diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEI&B) remain critical, resource constraints may challenge progress. Organizations need to double down on DEI&B efforts despite these limitations.
  5. Mental Health: Mental well-being is paramount, with employee stress and burnout reaching critical levels. Dedicated mental health initiatives are essential.
  6. Trust in Leadership: Trust in leadership is at an all-time low. Transparent, honest communication from leaders is vital to rebuild trust and navigate organizational changes.
  7. HR Transformation: HR itself is undergoing transformation, focusing on agility and new skills, particularly in AI and data-driven decision-making.
  8. Compensation Spotlight: Economic uncertainties and regulatory changes are pushing compensation into the spotlight. Pay equity and transparency are more important than ever.
  9. Sustainability: Sustainability has moved beyond compliance to become a core element of organizational strategy, influencing HR policies and employee engagement.

Why Employee Experience (still) Matters

Employee experience is not just a buzzword; it is fundamental to organizational success. At its core, employee experience encompasses the beliefs, emotions, and attitudes that employees develop based on their job interactions. It shapes how employees perceive their roles, work environment, and interactions with the organization.

Managing employee experience involves more than creating great experiences; it requires managing perceptions and expectations. For example, perceptions about pay fairness can significantly impact employee satisfaction. Addressing these perceptions through transparent communication and equitable practices is crucial.

Employee experience also drives behaviour. For instance, implementing a self-service system may reduce administrative burden but can frustrate employees if it is confusing. Therefore, focusing on user-friendly design and clear communication is essential.

Organizations must balance technology and human psychology to create positive employee experiences. This means understanding what makes a great day at work and how technology can support these experiences. For example, providing tools that simplify tasks, offering development opportunities, and recognizing achievements can enhance employee satisfaction.

In times of change, such as the shift to hybrid work or the integration of AI, managing employee experience is even more critical. Employees seek meaning and purpose in their work, and organizations must deliver by fostering a supportive and engaging environment. Addressing issues like quiet quitting requires a focus on providing positive experiences to maintain engagement.

How to (re)create Employee Experience Journey

Creating a positive employee experience journey involves understanding the needs and expectations of employees and designing processes and tools that meet these needs. SAP SuccessFactors offers a structured approach to help organizations achieve this through their advisory and design services: 

Module 1: Discover

The journey begins with the "Discover" module, where organizations explore the concept of experience management. Design thinking workshops help identify and prioritize moments that matter to employees. The outcome is a prioritized list of these moments, forming the basis for further design.

Module 2: Design

In the "Design" module, organizations define and specify personas for the employee experience journey. Through focus groups, interviews, and surveys, they assess the current state and develop a future state experience journey. The goal is to co-create a vision with functional consultants, ensuring alignment and shared understanding.

Module 3: Impact

The "Impact" module involves pre-planning workshops with stakeholders to align on the vision and implementation. This ensures that different workstreams are coordinated and aimed at achieving the desired employee experience.

SAP SuccessFactors Customer Examples driving successful re-design 

Global Customer: 360 Feedback Tool

A global customer implemented a 360 feedback tool for employee development but faced low usage. Through expert interviews and focus groups, they identified technical and perception-based barriers. The solution involved creating a comprehensive 360 feedback tool, a lighter version for targeted feedback, and an instant feedback tool for project milestones. These improvements were based on employee and manager input, enhancing the tool's usability and acceptance.

Global Retailer: Feedback and Recognition

For a global retailer, fostering a feedback culture in a shop floor environment was challenging. Limited time for focus groups led to a short survey collecting manager and employee input. Based on the survey results, personas and experience journeys were created, leading to the introduction of a continuous feedback tool accessible on mobile devices. This improved feedback culture among retail client advisors, crucial for engagement and retention.

Global Customer: Performance and Goals Tool 

A global customer struggled with the usability of their performance and goals tool. Design thinking sessions with employees, managers, and HR identified pain points and prioritized improvements. The result was a more intuitive and user-friendly tool, enhancing the performance management experience.

Conclusion

Employee experience is a critical component of HR strategy, impacting engagement, productivity, and retention. By understanding and addressing the needs and perceptions of employees, organizations can create positive and meaningful experiences. SAP SuccessFactors provides a framework to help organizations design and implement effective employee experience journeys, ensuring alignment with business goals and employee expectations.

Full webinar recording

Slides

If you'd like to learn more about SAP SuccessFactors Services projects or explore Employe Experience for your organization, please do not hesitate to contact HR Innovation & Transformation Team: saphit@sap.com or reach out to Nicole Schadewald directly: nicole.schadewald@sap.com

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration into SAP BW/4HANA
entry.content:
yes i agree with  @benjamin_gierden2 

 

@FrankRiesner SAP PO or CPI cannot connect with  bw4hana outbound adso. It should either be have a medium inbetween like wasb or staging.

1) BW4HANA (ADSO) -> SAP CPI / PO - not possible because the request work for push based mechanism and not pull based.

2) BW4HANA (Odata) -> SAP CPI / PO - not really good in terms of performance especially with CPI.

CC: @FrankRiesner  @benjamin_gierden2  @mariepotteau  @jassimparkar  @RolandKramer @

entry.title: How to mainatin storage costs code in HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

we would like to customize and use an optimized lot size procedure. An essential part within this procedure is ist storage costs code assigned in the material master. 

Usually this code can be maintained within the customizing activity of the lot size procedure (HANA on premise). But when I try this in cloud, the option to maintain the storage costs code is not visible. How can it be maintained though?

Best regards

Christoph

entry.title: Ability to Pin Learning Journey
entry.content:
Is there a way to mark a Learning Journey Favorite or Pin the Learning Journeys or courses that I want to Pursue in future or the selected courses to my Personal Learning Path ?

Also, as of now, I do not see a differentiator for completed courses and new ones, only when I open the Learning Journey then I see the Units completed. There should be a indicator which informs the users about completed courses when they are returned in search.

Any plans to add above features ?

 Sujit.

entry.title: How to print a pdf cheque form PMW?
entry.content:
Hello, 
We have developed a PDF based form to print our Cheque. Our pdf form is ZFIFAE850015_CHECK_UK. 

I'm facing an issue when trying to print this form. I read on internet that we should use program SAPFPAYM for Cheque PDF form printing. But when executing this program, I get error message Form ZFIFAE850015_CHECK_UK cannot be used for output management (Message No. BFIBL02702). 
With sapscript form, the program is RFFOUS_C. 

My questions are: 

  1. Can we print cheque form in ADOBE PDF?
  2. If yes, what is the program to print cheque form? Is it program SAPFPAYM?

entry.title: How to see message by using <xsl:message terminate="true" in XSLT mapping
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a problem with an XSLT mapping in the Integration Suite:

I want to check for the existence of some mandatory fields in the mapping and, if they are missing, abort the mapping and throw an exception.

Normally, this works using the instruction `<xsl:message terminate="yes">Exception Text</xsl:message>`.

The mapping is aborted in the Integration Suite, but I can't find my message "Exception Text" anywhere. I can only see the error message "Processing terminated by xsl:message at line 5 in XSLTMapping1.xsl," but not my message.

Does anyone know how to see the message in the log?
Otherwise nobody can't see the reason for the cancellation of the mapping.

Kind regards
Thorsten

entry.title: Re: The value of the 'Tax' output parameter of the formul...

entry.title: SAP Ariba: Export of Ariba Data - Data Warehouse (Data Strategy)
entry.content:
Hello ~ I am reaching out with this question to the SAP Ariba SCM community. We are using SAP Ariba Sourcing, Contracts, Buying, and Invoicing and are exploring options to export the data and land it in our data warehouse. This was attempted a couple of years ago with the creation of templates and exporting data via Analytical API. However, there are challenges with this approach: 1) Keeping Ariba record changes and deletions in synch with the DW; 2) Analytical API limits are reached due to high volumes of data; and 3) Templates that contain vector fields have a lower Analytical API limit (5,000 per day). Also, as an FYI, our ERP system is PeopleSoft Financials.
If you have successfully implemented an SAP Ariba data strategy with the export of data to a DW, I would really appreciate a response to this post. We are open to all ideas - internal middleware development using SAP Ariba APIs, SAP solutions, non-SAP solutions, etc. Thank you!

entry.title: Is there conflicts between subcontracting process and STO when transfer pricing function actived?
entry.content:
we're going to setup S4H ERP system. we want to transfer price function and it has been actived now. we have a business process scenario that subcontracting for intra-company. The consultant told us that transfer price function needs STO for intra-company and STO will conflict with subcontracting for intra-company. Could you let me know about solution among functions which transfer price, STO and subcontract? looking forward your reply. thank you very much. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there conflicts between subcontracting process and...
entry.content:
Please go through the below help portal to get more information.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/91b21005dded4984bcccf4a69ae1300c/c860bd534f22b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: How can I clear a draft of a canceled document from the Approval process
entry.content:
Hello Gurus, 

My client has an approval stage set up, and needs to change the list of Authorizers due to company turnover. 

When removing that person from the list of Authorizers and adding the intended replacement, we receive the following red band error: 

Cannot update approval stage; existing authorization processes are based on current stage

So, navigating to Administration -> Approval Process -> Approval Status Report, and entering the following Selection Criteria: 

With Template From: and Template To: specifying the Approval Stage that needs to be altered.

In the results, I see Purchase Order Document Draft 51526 listed. The Document draft is listed as Status: Approved, and when opened, above the header the document reads "Purchase Order -  Draft for Document Update [Approved].

When opening Purchase Order 51526, that document is canceled.

Reviewing the Change Log, I see that in History Instance 2, above the document header the document reads "Purchase Order - Document Update [Pending]", and by History Instance 3, the document was canceled. 

Now, to my question: How can I clear this Approved draft of a Canceled document so that the Approval Stage can be modified? 

Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Custom response in CREATE/READ handler of entity
entry.content:
Create your custom server.js file under srv folder, create a custom express.js app in this server. and call this server.js file on bootstrap. then you will be able to use express library in cap handler.

it is easy. that is why cap is famous where you not only can use beauty of CAP framework but also all functionalities of express.js.

If you implement this, you can handle any payload. No entities are required nothing is required only define an action in service definition and write handler in srv file.

entry.title: Re: Embedded TM / Seafreight Booking question
entry.content:
Hi @philippbergthaler ,

yes, the order allows order combination and partial deliveries. I debugged it and identified that TM only identifies the seafreight booking as freight order. Actually, I expected that the pick-up freight order is relevant (= what it is in reality). 

my scenario:

  • seafreight booking: FU A + FU B (both from the same sales order)
    • pick up order 1 for FU A
    • pick up order 2 for FU B

I need a delivery per pick up order.

TM creates one delivery. 😯

All the discussions I found about the delivery profile seems not valid for my case because I understood that the delivery profile is only relevant for UNPLANNED freight units. In my case, the freight units are well planned. 

Is there any advice how this process can be realized? I think it's not from outer space. 😉

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to play videos in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hey there!

You can use the Video Banner Component from SmartEdit in order to play videos in SAP Commerce Cloud. How to do so, you can refer to our documentation on the topic:  Video Banner Component.

Kind Regards,
Wesley

entry.title: Re: How to mainatin storage costs code in HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Christoph

 

In an OnPremise the storage costs code can be customised through the transaction OMI4

 

In S/4HANA Cloud OMI4 is available as the configuration activity Check Lot-Sizing Procedures 101112 but unfortunately the customizing to maintain storage costs code has not been exposed in it

 

This is missing functionality , please submit a feature request if you would like it added in a future release

Best regards

Enda

entry.title: How to do data modelling of 200+ plus tables in IDT in one universe?
entry.content:
The requirement is to include 200 plus tables in one universe. How do we generally join so many tables and get a complete view of the tables? If we create custom views, then will it be a part of a Master view. 

Also, how can we edit the joins later? because so many joins will make it look very clumsy.

 

SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Standard Unit of measur...
entry.content:
Hi @borah 

My understanding the standard units of measure are missing there translation when using Turkish logon.

 

The standard units of measure are not changeable as you have found . This is a known issue that requires a support ticket with SAP to be created ( On the component  BC-CUS-TOL-BCD ) and they should fix the issue

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud Profitability Report
entry.content: adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Finance

entry.title: Re: y2ysync Datahub cronjob is stuck in RUNNING status. C...
entry.content:
Hello there!

Cronjobs getting stuck in RUNNING state can have different reasons and ways to resolve this situation.

Therefore, kindly check below KBAs on the topic and follow its recommendations in order to prevent it from happening regardless the origin:

  • 3114125 - Business processes stuck in Running status 
  • 3151717 - How to troubleshoot issues where Business Processes and scheduled CronJobs are not triggered 
  • 3423117 - Cronjob stuck in RUNNING status after pod crash 

Kind Regards,
Wesley

entry.title: Re: How to create a new role in SAP commerce
entry.content:
Hello there!

Indeed the scenario you are trying to achieve is done by using User Groups and managing them with restrictions. How to do so and further information is explained in below documentations on the subject. Take look:

  • Managing Users and User Groups 
  • User Group Management 
  • Managing Customer User Groups in Backoffice 
  • Creating User Group Restrictions 

Kind Regards,
Wesley

entry.title: Re: Fiori List Report displays identical records despite...
entry.content:
Hi Angeliya, are those entries complete duplicates or only for the date/time? 

Do you have duplicates for other times as well? Check out the binding of the rows with UI5 Inspector

entry.title: Re: Unable to "Create new content slot for a Page" throug...
entry.content:
Hey There!

To add a new content slot it is expected to create a new page or edit an existent one with its template updated with the additional content slot. It is not expected to create additional content slots through Backoffice, only manage them.

This and more information can be found in the KBA 3091330 - How to add new Content Slots in SmartEdit - SAP Commerce.

Kind Regards,
Wesley

entry.title: How to add custom Image Logo on sfc card
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Is there any way to add image to standard sfc card , where we can insert an Image logo at highlighted area 

 

Thanks in advance,

regards,

Anvesh

entry.title: How can I display the original receipt used for returning articles on CCO?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a customer who wants to be able to see on their Invoice doc on SAP the original receipt ID when returning a receipt.

I noticed there is a field on CCO (Original receipt ID) for this information, but it is blank and I can't see how to activate that.

Any ideas how can I get this field filled?

Thank you all!

entry.title: Re: Sales Order with 2 line items only confirms the 1st,...
entry.content:
Thanks for your response;

Sorry, I should have figured this out sooner; the issue was due to Safety stock; the material had an stock that was less than the safety stock, so the aujtomatick check would not take place in this case.

 

Thanks again1

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
Please bring back the links to optional surveys.  Many companies have already voted for bringing back this feature: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/325714/?section=sectionDetails

We don't want to be strict to the point of not granting people completion on the basis of survey but we do want to encourage people as much as possible to take the optional survey.  It does not help if there are no longer links on the new home to access surveys.

entry.title: Re: The value of the 'Tax' output parameter of the formul...
entry.content:
Hi, @Felipe_Lima,

It was a test, but it didn't work

entry.title: Re: How does hybris hash passwords?
entry.content:
Hey There!

These are our documentations on the topic regarding how the password are hashed in SAP Commerce Cloud:

  • New Default Password Hashing Algorithm 
  • Password Storage Strategies 

Kind Regards,
Wesley

entry.title: Re: What are the Media Storage Strategy in Hybris Commerc...
entry.content:
Hey There!

The correct answer is number 1 "Local File media storage strategy" and 3 "Azure Media Storage strategy".

Kind Regards,
Wesley

entry.title: HANA restore delta without log backups
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a client running Full HANA database backups on their DEV&QAS systems over weekends and Delta backups nightly.  Log mode is set to Overwrite.

DEV need to be restored to last night (Monday Night)

HANA catalog is available.  However, After selecting the full backup in "Restore Database" operation, the Delta backup is not available for selection.

I notice following from internet:

I restored database, and selected "restore database to latest point in time":

This worked.   Database was successfully restored to last night - restore used the Delta backup!

Confirmed, as table E070 contains all transports created until last night.

backup.log file of restore attached.

Is the HANA documentation incorrect, as I was able to restore to the last Delta backup?

Log backups use lots of space, but seems it is best to enable log backups also for DEV and QAS systems, as a Delta backup restore without log backups might be problematic?

Your feedback will be appreciated.

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
I have submitted an influence suggestion for newly added courses to be available immediately on new home page instead of 6-24 hours: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/324540/?section=sectionDetails

Someone also made a comment on the influence idea that this suggestion has been submitted multiple times, is there any plan to address this?  

The new home page is supposed to be an improvement over the old home page but right now it seems to be making us go backwards--we are losing functionalities that we already have today?

entry.title: Table Partitioning
entry.content:
Please provide commands to view and drop range partitions

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Table Partitioning
entry.content:
The documentation on Range Partitions is here:  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_IQ/a8937bea84f21015a80bc776cf758d50/a6faa17f84f21015ae6faa41dfa4c230.html

The system view SYSPARTITION contains information about existing partitions: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_IQ/a898e08b84f21015969fa437e89860c8/a5d44e9984f210159983d6f3d800d5db.html

The difference actions on partitions are described here:  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_IQ/e2667dfb2dd0428f9180cc1cbfed2f79/146e0158751e1014a263aa258901c23f.html
